articles.title: PAYPAY, 40% OWNER OF BINANCE JAPAN, SEEKS UP TO $1.1 BILLION IN IPO
articles.summary: Japan's largest cashless provider, PayPay, plans a $1.1 billion Nasdaq IPO targeting a $10 billion valuation. Backed by SoftBank, the firm owns 40% of Binance Japan. The listing integrates digital payments with crypto, allowing 70 million users to fund purchases via Binance Japan using PayPay Money.

articles.title: SPACEX'S $780 MILLION BTC STACK DOWN TO ~$545 MILLION AHEAD OF IPO FILING
articles.summary: SpaceX holds 8,285 bitcoin worth $545 million, reflecting a $235 million paper loss over three months. As the company prepares a $50 billion IPO and $1.75 trillion valuation, its S-1 filing will expose investors to crypto volatility, mirroring Tesla's previous balance sheet risks and headline challenges.

articles.title: CUSTOM STABLECOINS BACKED BY PAYPAL USD
articles.summary: M0, MoonPay, and PayPal have launched PYUSDx, a white-label stablecoin-as-a-service product that allows companies to issue branded stablecoins collateralized by PYUSD, combining M0's reserve management infrastructure, MoonPay's fiat onramp rails, and PayPal's PYUSD as the underlying reserve asset. This positions PYUSD as a B2B reserve layer rather than purely a consumer-facing instrument.

articles.title: VITALIK BUTERIN OUTLINES PLAN TO OVERHAUL ETHEREUM'S EXECUTION LAYER
articles.summary: Vitalik Buterin's two-part proposal aims to modernize Ethereum's execution layer by implementing binary state trees and transitioning beyond the current Ethereum Virtual Machine. These changes target improved scalability and efficiency, fundamentally restructuring how the network processes transactions and manages state data for long-term sustainability.

articles.title: VITALIK ON EIP-8141
articles.summary: EIP-8141 is an omnibus account abstraction proposal built around "Frame Transactions" – bundled call sequences that share calldata and can independently authorize both a transaction sender and a gas payer, enabling paymaster contracts to settle fees in any token via onchain DEX logic with no intermediaries. It addresses quantum-resistant and multisig validation, ZK-SNARK paymasters for privacy protocols, 2D nonces for parallel transaction processing, and a conservative-to-permissive mempool rollout designed to complement FOCIL's inclusion guarantees. EOA compatibility is under active discussion, which would extend batch operations and gas sponsorship to existing accounts without migration. EIP-8141 could ship within a year via the Hegota fork, culminating over a decade of AA research since EIP-86 in 2016.

articles.title: HUMANITY'S ORIGINAL STORE OF VALUE
articles.summary: Gold's decoupling from real rates beginning in late 2022 – rallying from $1,620 to $5,200/oz while real yields held at 1.5-2.5% – indicates a structural regime shift attributed to fiscal dominance, with US debt adding roughly $8B/day and net interest costs projected to exceed the defense budget in 2026. Central banks absorbed 863 tonnes in 2025 alone, yet Western institutional gold allocation remains near 1% of ETF and fund assets versus a historically appropriate 5-10%. Over the comparable window in which gold gained 78%, BTC reached an ATH of $126K before retracing 50% to approximately $67K, and the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve's $13B contrasts sharply with $140B+ in annual central bank gold purchasing.

articles.title: QUANTUM RESISTANCE ROADMAP FOR ETHEREUM
articles.summary: Vitalik Buterin has published a comprehensive quantum resistance roadmap addressing four attack surfaces — consensus-layer BLS signatures, data availability, EOA signatures, and application-layer ZK proofs — with distinct cryptographic migration paths for each. The most acute challenge is at the ZK layer, where quantum-resistant STARKs carry ~10M gas cost versus 300-500K for current SNARKs. The unifying solution is protocol-layer recursive aggregation via EIP-8141 validation frames, in which a single STARK per block could replace 3-256MB of per-transaction signatures and proofs, with proving delegated to the mempool layer at roughly 500ms intervals. A significant open question remains the selection of Ethereum's long-term hash function — candidates include Poseidon2 with extra rounds, Poseidon1, and BLAKE3.

articles.title: AGENTIC COMMERCE IS TAKING SHAPE
articles.summary: There are two competing payment rails for agentic commerce: an enterprise stack anchored by OpenAI's ACP (with Stripe) and Google's AP2 (60+ partners including Mastercard and PayPal) targeting human-assisted purchasing, and a crypto stack led by Coinbase's x402 protocol, which has processed 50M+ USDC transactions on Base and Solana with volume doubling in February. Coinbase launched Agentic Wallets with native x402 support, while Virtuals Protocol's ACP extends the crypto stack into agent-to-agent commerce via cryptographic Proof of Agreement and smart contract escrow. The fundamental wedge favoring crypto is settlement efficiency – stablecoins clear in seconds for fractions of a cent versus card rails at 2-3% with multi-day settlement. The predicted outcome is a bifurcated market where enterprise rails dominate human-to-merchant flows while crypto captures agent-to-agent commerce.

articles.title: SOFI ADDS SOLANA NETWORK DEPOSITS
articles.summary: SoFi, a national chartered bank that supports direct crypto custody, has enabled Solana network deposits directly in the SoFi app.

articles.title: SOMEONE JUST LAUNCHED A TOKEN FOR YOUR AGENT: A BREAKDOWN
articles.summary: Bankr enables community members to permissionlessly launch tokens for AI agents, automatically routing approximately 1% of all trade volume to a wallet tied to the agent's X handle.

articles.title: VITALIK BUTERIN EYES ‘BIG FOCIL' AND ENCRYPTED MEMPOOLS
articles.summary: The Glamsterdam upgrade will boost Ethereum's censorship-resistance, but a proposed mechanism called ePBS could cause centralization.

articles.title: EMPOWERING AI AGENTS WITH ON-CHAIN AML RISK ANALYSIS CAPABILITIES
articles.summary: SlowMist launched MistTrack Skills to provide AI agents with automated AML risk analysis.
